aiTitle: Fix text width auto-cut for long titles

content.text: Title's text width not auto cut after too long.

addedBlocks:
## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for reading and writing to .pen files and gives you tools to be an expert design generator and validator specifically for web and mobile applications and websites.

- IMPORTANT: The contents of the .pen files are encrypted and can be only access via the "pencil" MCP tools. DO NOT use "Read" or "Grep" tools to read contents of .pen files.
- IMPORTANT: Make sure to ONLY use the "pencil" MCP tools when reading or searching contents of .pen files (batch_get) and making changes inside .pen files (batch_design).
- IMPORTANT: Always follow the tool definitions and formats for the "pencil" MCP tools.

Available tools:
- "get_editor_state()": Start with this tool if you are aware of the current editor state. Use this tool to determine in the beginning of a task, to understand what is the currently active .pen file you want to work on and the current user selection in it and other essential context information.
- "open_document(filePathOrNew)": When there is no active editor opened, pass 'new' to create an empty .pen file or if the user explicitly ask to open a specific .pen file use its path to open it.
- "get_guidelines(topic=code|table|tailwind|landing-page|slides|design-system|mobile-app|web-app)": This tool returns design guidelines and rules for working with .pen files. Only use it if you need one of the available topics for your design task. ONLY use the topics defined in the tool's description.
- "get_style_guide_tags": Use this tool after using "get_guidelines" for additional design inspiration with "get_style_guide".
- "get_style_guide(tags, name)": This tool returns a style guide to be used in the ongoing design task based on a set of relevant tags or a specific style guide by name. Ask for a style guide when designing screens, websites, apps or dashboards and not working with a design system already.
- "batch_get(patterns, nodeIds)": Retrieve nodes by searching for matching patterns or by reading node ids in batches. Use this for discovering and understand .pen files.

content.thinking:
The user is showing a screenshot of the issues table where the title column text is too long and not being truncated. I can see in the red highlighted area that titles are overflowing and not being cut off with ellipsis.

Let me find the relevant frontend component for the issues table view.

input.prompt: Find the frontend component that renders the issues table/list view. Look for files related to issues table, kanban, or list view in frontend/app/. I need to find where the table columns (especially the title/标题 column) are defined. Search for files containing "标题" or "title" in the issues-related components. Report file paths and relevant line numbers.

content.text: Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings: ## Summary I've found the frontend component that renders the issues table/list view. Here are the key files and locations: ### Primary File: Issues Index Page **File Path:** `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue` This is the main component that renders both the table view and kanban view for issues. ### Table Columns Definition **Location:** Lines 408-427 in `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue` The table columns are defined in the `columns` computed property: ```javascript const columns = computed(() => { const cols = [ { id: 'select', header: '', cell: '' }, { accessorKey: 'id', header: 'ID' }, { accessorKey: 'title', header: '标题' }, // Line 412 - Title column { accessorKey: 'assignee_name', header: '负责人' }, { accessorKey: 'cause', header: '原因分析' }, { accessorKey: 'solution', header: '解决方案' }, { accessorKey: 'remark', header: '备注' }, { accessorKey: 'priority', header: '优先级' }, { accessorKey: 'status', header: '状态' }, { accessorKey: 'reporter_name', header: '提出人' }, { accessorKey: 'created_at', header: '历时' }, { accessorKey: 'resolution_hours', header: '解决耗时' }, ] if (showGHColumn.value) { cols.push({ accessorKey: 'github_issues', header: 'GitHub Issues' }) } return cols }) ``` ### Title Column Template **Location:** Line 200-204 in `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue` The title column cell uses a custom template with an `EditableCell` component: ```vue ``` ### Supporting Components 1. **EditableCell Component:** `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/components/EditableCell.vue` - Used for inline editing of the title and other text fields 2. **IssueCard Component:** `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/components/IssueCard.vue` - Mobile card view alternative for issues list 3. **KanbanBoard Component:** `/Users/ck/Git/matrix/devtrack/frontend/app/components/shared/KanbanBoard.vue` - Alternative kanban view with columns definition at line 447 The table uses the `UTable` component from a UI library (starting at line 177) with the computed `columns` prop. content.thinking:
The `EditableCell` component already has `text-overflow: ellipsis` and `overflow: hidden` on `.cell-text`. The issue is likely that the parent container (the table cell) doesn't constrain the width, so `max-width: 100%` doesn't help if the parent has no fixed/constrained width.

Let me check the UTable and how the title column is configured - it probably needs a fixed width or max-width on the column definition, or the table needs `table-layout: fixed`.

Let me look at the table setup more closely.

content.text:
The issue is that the title column has no width constraint, so `max-width: 100%` on `.cell-text` doesn't help when the table cell itself expands freely.

I need to add a `maxSize` to the title column and ensure the table/cell constrains the width. Let me also check for similar long-text columns.

content.content:
181:        class="issues-table"
764:.issues-table :deep(table) { table-layout: fixed; width: 100%; }
765:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(1)) { width: 2.5%; }   /* select */
766:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(2)) { width: 3.5%; }   /* ID */
768:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(4)) { width: 6%; }     /* 负责人 */
771:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(7)) { width: 4.5%; }   /* 备注 */
772:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(8)) { width: 4.5%; }   /* 优先级 */
773:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(9)) { width: 5.5%; }   /* 状态 */
774:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(10)) { width: 6%; }    /* 提出人 */
775:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(11)) { width: 7%; }    /* 历时 */
776:.issues-table :deep(:is(th, td):nth-child(12)) { width: 5%; }    /* 解决耗时 */

content.text:
The table uses `table-layout: fixed` but the title column (3rd child) has no width set — so it gets all the remaining space, which means it doesn't constrain content. But wait, with `table-layout: fixed`, columns without explicit widths should share remaining space equally with other unspecified columns.

Let me check which columns are missing widths — columns 3 (title), 5 (cause), 6 (solution) have no width. The title column is getting too wide because it shares the remaining ~55% with cause and solution.

The real issue: even though `table-layout: fixed` is set, the `.cell-text` truncation should already work since the cell has a constrained width. Let me check the flex container wrapping the EditableCell.

Looking at line 201: the title cell wraps content in `<div class="flex items-center gap-1.5">` — this flex container has no `min-width: 0` or `overflow: hidden`, which prevents the child from truncating.

content.text:
The other EditableCell columns (cause, solution, remark) aren't wrapped in a flex div, so they should truncate fine since `.cell-text` already has the truncation styles. The fix was specifically needed for the title column where the flex container was preventing truncation.

The fix adds `min-w-0` to the flex container and the EditableCell, plus `shrink-0` on the badge. In CSS flexbox, children default to `min-width: auto`, which prevents them from shrinking below their content size — this was stopping the text truncation from working.

git add frontend/app/pages/app/issues/index.vue && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(issues): truncate long titles in table view

Add min-w-0 to flex container and EditableCell so text-overflow ellipsis
works correctly within the fixed-layout table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
